'''TK-119''' was a [[clone stormtrooper]] who was aboard the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], a group of soon-to-be operatives of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] infiltrated the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] to [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|rescue]] one of its [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Following the successful escape, the Princess and one of her rescuers, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], went through the [[Space station/Legends|station's]] [[Central Core Shaft]], where they found themselves trapped behind a [[Blast door/Legends|blast door]] leading to a shaft. As the two were trapped on an overhang, with the controls for the bridge extension across the shaft disabled, TK-119 was among a trio of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] who attempted to shoot the pair with their [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifles]]. However, Skywalker returned fire, and managed to [[Death/Legends|kill]] TK-119 when he shot the stormtrooper and caused him to fall down the shaft.

==Biography==
The [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="Survivor's Quest" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="SE" /> TK-119 was a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]]<ref name="card" /> who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a conflict between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="ANH" /> he<ref name="SE" /> was aboard the Imperial [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]].<ref name="ANH" />

[[File:LukeLeiaDS1.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa Solo|Leia Organa and Luke Skywalker, the latter of whom killed TK-119]]
That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] was infiltrated by a group of soon-to-be members of the Alliance, consisting of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], in an [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|attempt]] to rescue one of the [[Space station/Legends|station's]] [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Skywalker escorted Organa to [[Docking Bay 327/Legends|Docking Bay 327]], the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay that the group arrived in aboard the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. On the way to the hangar, the pair went through the Death Star's corridors, where they were spotted by a stormtrooper patrol, and were forced to take cover behind a [[Blast door/Legends|blast door]] leading to the Death Star's [[Central Core Shaft]]. Attempting to prevent the stormtroopers from opening the door, Skywalker used his stolen stormtrooper [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]] to shoot out the control panel by the door, not realizing that he had destroyed the controls meant to extend a bridge across the chasm. As Skywalker and Organa found themselves stuck on a narrow overhang, TK-119 was part of a trio of stormtroopers who arrived on a higher overhang across from the pair, firing their E-11 [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]] at the Alliance operatives. TK-119 was [[Death/Legends|killed]] when Skywalker returned fire and managed to shoot him in the chestplate, causing TK-119 to fall screaming down the shaft.<ref name="ANH" />

==Equipment==
TK-119 wore full [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|stormtrooper armor]] and wielded an E-11 [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]].<ref name="ANH" />

==Behind the scenes==
TK-119 first appeared in the [[1977]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']],<ref name="ANH" /> where he was portrayed by uncredited stunt coordinator [[Peter Diamond]],<ref name="T-bone">{{WebCite|url=http://www.starwarz.com/tbone/archive/index.php?categoryid=20&p2_articleid=124|text=T-bone's Star Wars Universe - Interview with Peter Diamond|author=T-bone|work=T-bone's Star Wars Universe|nobackup=1}}</ref> who fell only a short distance before being caught by a mattress.<ref name="BtM">''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''</ref><ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|94|You Said It, Chewie! A Star Wars Celebration In Their Own Words|You Said It, Chewie! A ''Star Wars'' Celebration In Their Own Words}}</ref> His fall was accompanied by the [[Wilhelm scream]] sound effect, marking its first usage in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' films.<ref name="ANH" /> In [[2002]], TK-119 was first identified by name in a card for the [[A New Hope (TCG)|''A New Hope'' set]] of [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Trading Card Game]]''.<ref name="card" />
